source_pr.title: store/tikv: fix coprocessor iterator goroutine leak.

symptoms: rss, err = tk.Se.Execute("select * from copclient order by id") (executor/distsql_test.go)
failure_signature: rss, err = tk.Se.Execute("select * from copclient order by id") (executor/distsql_test.go)
root_cause_explanation: The flaky behavior was caused by a goroutine leak in the TiKV coprocessor iterator path: after closing the result set, the background 'copIterator' goroutine could keep running. Leaked goroutines can pollute subsequent tests (resource contention, timeouts, order-dependent failures). This PR fixes it by adjusting the iterator loop to stop correctly after finishing/cancelation and by adding/adjusting a regression test that verifies the goroutine disappears after Close().
fix_pattern: Ensure iterator/background worker goroutines exit on Close() to prevent goroutine leaks that can pollute later tests; add regression checks for leaked goroutines when closing result sets.

evidence: evidence: rss, err = tk.Se.Execute("select * from copclient order by id") (executor/distsql_test.go)
